data.title: Build an AI operating system without making the team slower
data.subtitle: A YouTube-sized explainer for turning scattered experiments into repeatable work.

sections.headline: Start with a workflow map, not a model menu
sections.body: List every handoff, decision, approval, and exception. The best automation target is usually hiding between two teams.

sections.headline: Give the system a narrow promise
sections.body: A small reliable workflow beats a broad demo. Define the output, the owner, the fallback, and the failure behavior.

sections.headline: Make review visible
sections.body: Humans should not have to reverse-engineer what happened. Put evidence, confidence, and exceptions in the path of work.

sections.headline: Measure rework, not novelty
sections.body: The operating metric is whether work gets cleaner, faster, and easier to trust after the system ships.

data.cta: Ship one workflow the team can trust.
